texto: In matters of conscience and basic convictions it is unlawful and pernicious for anyone to forcibly intrude upon another's beliefs; therefore, because I am a man of rational convictions, I will not go out and demolish churches, drown monks, or rip down icons from my friends' walls because in so doing I will not spread my convictions; human beings must be educated, not coerced, I am not the enemy, I am not the tyrant of the conscience of true believers.
explicacao: Orientação direta de N.G. Pomyalovsky sobre temas ligados a amizade. A afirmação “In matters of conscience and basic convictions it is unlawful and pernicious for anyone to forcib…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, atheism, christianity, útil em momentos de termino e christianity.

seo.titleSeo: "In matters of conscience and basic convictions it is…" — N.G. Pomyalovsky | Metamensagem
seo.descriptionSeo: Orientação direta de N.G. Pomyalovsky sobre temas ligados a amizade. A afirmação “In matters of conscience and basic convictions it is unlawful and pernicious…

frase_original: In matters of conscience and basic convictions it is unlawful and pernicious for anyone to forcibly intrude upon another's beliefs; therefore, because I am a man of rational convictions, I will not go out and demolish churches, drown monks, or rip down icons from my friends' walls because in so doing I will not spread my convictions; human beings must be educated, not coerced, I am not the enemy, I am not the tyrant of the conscience of true believers.

texto: These ordained atheists cultivate their egoism, the source of every atheist's activity, but they defile it and it becomes repulsive whereas the egoism of good atheists is a beautiful principle. They preach raging sermons not because they fear for the eternal damnation of their fold, but because they fear for the eternal damnation of their gold; before every sermon they feel their pockets to see if there's a hole, and if there is, they mend it with a sermon instead of a patch.
explicacao: Citação de N.G. Pomyalovsky sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “These ordained atheists cultivate their egoism, the source of every atheist's activity, but they …” articula coragem, atheism, christianity em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como termino e christianity.

They preach raging sermons not because they fear for the eternal damnation of their fold, but because they fear for the eternal damnation of their gold; before every sermon they feel their pockets to see if there's a hole, and if there is, they mend it with a sermon instead of a patch.

texto: You will find complete, absolute atheism, not a conscious atheism, but rather the animal atheism of an uneducated man, the atheism of a cat or dog. They call themselves believers, and they lie: they neither believe in nor rely upon that God to whom woman, children, idealists, and people in misfortune like to turn.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a N.G. Pomyalovsky: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “You will find complete, absolute atheism, not a conscious atheism, but rather the animal atheism …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, atheism, christianity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de christianity.
